Title: Engineering the unicellular alga Phaeodactylum tricornutum for high-value plant triterpenoid production

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: High-value plant triterpenoid; intermediate en route to betulinic acid, a pharmaceutical precursor with reported anticancer and anti-HIV potential
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cell pellet; low betulin peak detected by GC-MS in transformant lines
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum/membrane-associated; truncated transmembrane domain removed in fusion construct
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in transgenic P. tricornutum lines under FCPA/LHCF1 promoter
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression with MtCYP716A12 and LjLUS in cotransformation lines; fusion protein engineering with MtCYP716A12-k-Δ71CPR; GC-MS metabolite profiling detecting betulin
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Photoautotrophic growth in F/2 medium; metabolite analysis at early/stationary phase; lab-scale and pilot-scale photobioreactors
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant growth defect observed in transformant lines at produced levels; betulin accumulation remained low
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR gene expression analysis; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtCPR was required as the redox partner for heterologous MtCYP716A12 in P. tricornutum. 2. Coexpression or fusion of MtCPR with MtCYP716A12 enabled oxidation of lupeol to betulin. 3. A truncated MtCPR lacking the N-terminal membrane anchor was successfully used in a CYP716A12 fusion strategy.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables betulin production when paired with MtCYP716A12 in lupeol-producing P. tricornutum lines
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory gene for reconstruction of plant triterpenoid oxidation modules in algae; fusion with CYP may improve electron transfer efficiency in chassis with limited promoters/markers
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not itself the catalytic triterpene-forming enzyme; included as a core engineered component because experimental Results centered on its necessity in the introduced oxidation module

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: High-value triterpene precursor for betulin/betulinic acid and other lupane-type compounds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cell pellet and medium; lupeol detected intracellularly and released into medium
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest transcript abundance in the best-producing line LjLUS-25; expression peaks at mid-exponential growth phase under FCPA/LHCF1 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable nuclear overexpression in P. tricornutum; GC-MS identification of lupeol; qRT-PCR expression analysis; copy-number analysis; time-course metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth phase strongly affected production, with maximum lupeol at mid-exponential phase; photoautotrophic culture in F/2 medium
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; functions as the introduced branch-switch enzyme competing with native cycloartenol synthase for 2,3-oxidosqualene
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced brassicasterol levels in producing lines due to precursor competition; no significant growth penalty detected
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Heterologous expression of LjLUS in P. tricornutum enabled lupeol accumulation, representing the first successful pentacyclic triterpenoid production in an engineered microalga. 2. LjLUS produced lupeol without the 3β,20-dihydroxylupane by-product seen with AtLUS. 3. Higher lupeol productivity correlated with higher LjLUS mRNA abundance but not with transgene copy number. 4. LjLUS expression perturbed native mevalonate/sterol pathway transcript levels and lowered brassicasterol content.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lupeol accumulation and diverts sterol-pathway flux away from brassicasterol
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lead entry-point enzyme for algal production of lupane triterpenes; can be stacked with downstream oxidases and precursor-supply engineering (e.g., MVA pathway enhancement)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected over AtLUS because it generated only lupeol in P. tricornutum; best line was LjLUS-25 with ~0.1 mg/L over 2 days and >0.1 mg/g dry biomass

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene primarily to lupeol and also forms 3β,20-dihydroxylupane as a by-product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: High-value triterpene precursor for downstream lupane products
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in transgenic lines under FCPA/LHCF1 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable nuclear overexpression in P. tricornutum; GC-MS detection of lupeol and 3β,20-dihydroxylupane
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Photoautotrophic culture in F/2 medium; analyzed in early stationary phase and pilot-scale trials
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Produces an additional by-product (3β,20-dihydroxylupane), reducing product specificity; no significant growth penalty observed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtLUS expression in P. tricornutum enabled lupeol production. 2. Unlike LjLUS, AtLUS also generated the by-product 3β,20-dihydroxylupane, often at similar titres depending on culture conditions. 3. AtLUS lines could be cultivated at pilot scale and retained lupeol production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lupeol accumulation but with lower product specificity than LjLUS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative lupeol-forming OSC, but less desirable than LjLUS when product purity is important
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as comparative OSC; not the main optimization focus after LjLUS lines were found more specific

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate downstream oxidation product/intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes lupeol at the C28 position to form betulin, with further oxidation capability toward betulin aldehyde and betulinic acid; reported as multifunctional lupeol/α-amyrin/β-amyrin 28-oxidase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: High-value lupane triterpenoid intermediate and precursor of betulinic acid with pharmaceutical potential
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cell pellet; low-abundance peak detected by GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Membrane-associated endoplasmic reticulum enzyme; engineered fusion to truncated CPR for improved function
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in cotransformation lines or as MtCYP716A12-k-Δ71CPR fusion in LjLUS-25 background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable cotransformation with LjLUS and MtCPR; iterative transformation with MtCYP716A12-k-Δ71CPR fusion; GC-MS detection of betulin
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Photoautotrophic culture; metabolites analyzed at early stationary phase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Reduction/electron transfer support by CPR described as potentially rate limiting; CYP oxidation efficiency requires optimization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Betulin production remained low; no detectable growth defect at current production levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtCYP716A12 functioned in P. tricornutum to oxidize lupeol to betulin when provided with MtCPR. 2. Both cotransformation and CYP-CPR fusion strategies produced betulin-positive lines. 3. Betulin was the major detectable oxidation product, while full conversion to betulinic acid was not achieved at appreciable levels.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables oxidation of lupeol to betulin in engineered P. tricornutum
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key downstream tailoring enzyme for constructing algal sapogenin pathways; suitable for fusion-based engineering to improve electron transfer and pathway compactness
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected from literature and confirmed in-house in yeast as multifunctional 28-oxidase; only low-frequency betulin-positive transformants were obtained
